questions.scenario: Your Palette Gauge is at 45.
questions.question: Can you use Subtractive Palette?
options: Yes - but it's a DPS loss
questions.explanation: Subtractive Palette requires at least 50 Palette Gauge to activate. At 45, you need to continue your base combo to build more gauge.

questions.scenario: You have 5 White Paint stacks and just finished a combo.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Continue combo - more paint is better
Use Holy in White to spend a stack
questions.explanation: White Paint caps at 5 stacks. If you're at max, use Holy in White to spend a stack before your combo finisher grants another, or you'll waste paint.

questions.scenario: You just used Fire in Red.
questions.question: What comes next in the combo?
questions.explanation: The base combo is Fire in Red → Aero in Green → Water in Blue. Following this order builds both Palette Gauge and White Paint.

questions.scenario: You just used Living Muse to summon a creature.
questions.question: What proc do you receive?
questions.explanation: Using Living Muse to summon a creature grants the Black Paint proc, which enables the powerful Comet in Black spell.

questions.scenario: You need to move for a mechanic.
questions.question: Which ability lets you cast while moving?
questions.explanation: Holy in White is an instant cast spell. Use it and other instants (Comet in Black, Rainbow Drip) for movement.

questions.scenario: You want to use Living Muse but have no creature painted.
questions.question: What must you do first?
questions.explanation: You must first paint a Creature Motif (Pom, Wing, Claw, or Maw) on your canvas before you can use Living Muse to summon it.

questions.scenario: The countdown to pull is at 15 seconds.
questions.question: What should you be doing?
options: Paint all three canvases (Creature, Weapon, Landscape)
questions.explanation: Pre-pull preparation: paint all three canvases (Creature, Weapon, Landscape) so you have immediate access to all Muses when combat starts.

questions.scenario: You painted Hammer Motif on your weapon canvas.
questions.question: What does this enable?
questions.explanation: Hammer Motif on the weapon canvas enables Striking Muse, which activates the powerful 3-hit hammer combo.

questions.scenario: You painted Starry Sky Motif on your landscape canvas.
questions.question: What does this enable?
questions.explanation: Starry Sky Motif on the landscape canvas enables Starry Muse, your 2-minute party damage buff.

questions.scenario: Mid-fight, the boss goes untargetable for 10 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do during downtime?
options: Paint any canvases that are empty
questions.explanation: Use downtime to paint empty canvases. This prepares you for maximum damage when the boss becomes targetable again.

questions.scenario: You use Living Muse with a Pom Motif painted.
questions.question: What happens?
options: Summons Pom Muse + grants Black Paint
questions.explanation: Living Muse summons the painted creature (Pom Muse in this case) and grants the Black Paint proc for Comet in Black.

questions.scenario: Starry Muse has a 120 second cooldown.
questions.question: What type of ability is this?
questions.explanation: Starry Muse is a 2-minute party damage buff (5% damage for 20s). Align it with other raid buffs for maximum benefit.

questions.scenario: Starry Muse is active (party buff window).
questions.question: What should you prioritize during this window?
options: Use Living Muse, Comet, Hammer combo, Star Prism
questions.explanation: During Starry Muse, use all your high-damage abilities: Living Muse (for Comet), Striking Muse (hammer combo), and Star Prism.

questions.scenario: You activated Striking Muse with a Hammer Motif.
questions.question: What combo does this enable?
options: Hammer Stamp → Hammer Brush → Polishing Hammer
questions.explanation: Striking Muse enables the hammer combo: Hammer Stamp → Hammer Brush → Polishing Hammer. All three hits are instant.

questions.scenario: You have Black Paint proc from Living Muse.
questions.question: What ability should you use it on?
questions.explanation: Black Paint enables Comet in Black, one of your highest potency spells. Use it before the proc expires.

questions.scenario: Your Palette Gauge is at 75.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Subtractive Palette only requires 50 gauge. At 75, you should use it to avoid overcapping - the gauge continues building.

questions.scenario: You just activated Subtractive Palette.
questions.question: What's the correct combo order?
options: Thunder in Cyan → Stone in Yellow → Blizzard in Magenta
questions.explanation: After Subtractive Palette, use Thunder in Cyan → Stone in Yellow → Blizzard in Magenta. This combo builds toward Star Prism.

questions.scenario: You completed the full Cyan combo (Thunder → Stone → Blizzard).
questions.question: What ability becomes available?
questions.explanation: Completing the Cyan combo (Monochromatic Tones) enables Star Prism, your big finisher ability with high potency.

questions.scenario: Your Palette Gauge is at 95 and still building.
questions.question: What's the risk?
questions.explanation: At 95+ gauge, you risk overcapping. Any gauge generated beyond 100 is wasted. Use Subtractive Palette at 50+ to prevent this.

questions.scenario: You used Thunder in Cyan but need to move for a mechanic.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Continue combo - Stone and Blizzard are important
questions.explanation: Complete the Cyan combo even if you need to use instants between hits. Dropping mid-combo loses significant DPS and Star Prism access.

questions.scenario: You have both Black Paint (Comet ready) and 5 White Paint stacks.
questions.question: Which should you use first?
questions.explanation: Comet in Black has higher potency and is a proc that can expire. Use it first, then Holy in White to manage White Paint stacks.

questions.scenario: You see the Rainbow Drip buff on your bar.
questions.question: What is Rainbow Drip?
questions.explanation: Rainbow Drip is a proc that grants an instant high-potency spell. Watch for it and use before it expires.

questions.scenario: You have 3 White Paint stacks and no other procs.
questions.question: When should you use Holy in White?
options: When you need an instant for movement
questions.explanation: Holy in White is valuable for movement since it's instant. Use it when you need mobility or when approaching 5 stacks to avoid capping.

questions.scenario: You have Comet ready, Rainbow Drip proc, and 4 White Paint.
questions.question: What's the priority order?
questions.explanation: Priority: Comet in Black (highest potency, proc) > Rainbow Drip (high potency, proc) > Holy in White (consistent but lower priority).

questions.scenario: Your Black Paint proc has 3 seconds remaining.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Use Comet immediately before it expires
Let it expire - not worth interrupting
questions.explanation: Black Paint is a high-value proc. If it's about to expire, prioritize using Comet in Black immediately rather than losing it.

questions.scenario: Starry Muse just came off cooldown.
questions.question: What should you ensure before using it?
options: Have all canvases painted for burst
questions.explanation: Before Starry Muse, ensure you have canvases painted (especially Creature and Weapon) so you can use Living Muse and Striking Muse during the buff window.

questions.scenario: You're in your Starry Muse burst window.
questions.question: What makes the hammer combo valuable here?
options: 3 instant high-potency hits under raid buffs
questions.explanation: The hammer combo (Hammer Stamp → Brush → Polishing Hammer) deals 3 instant high-potency hits, all benefiting from Starry Muse's damage buff.

questions.scenario: A Dragoon announces Battle Litany is coming in 5 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do with Starry Muse?
options: Hold briefly to align with Battle Litany
questions.explanation: Starry Muse is a party buff. Aligning it with other raid buffs (like Battle Litany) multiplies the benefit for the entire party.

questions.scenario: Starry Muse is active. You have Comet ready and Star Prism available.
questions.question: What's the priority?
questions.explanation: Comet in Black is a proc that can expire. Use it first to ensure you don't lose it, then use Star Prism during the remaining buff duration.

questions.scenario: Mid-burst, you used Hammer Stamp.
questions.question: What must you do next?
options: Continue with Hammer Brush → Polishing Hammer
questions.explanation: Once you start the hammer combo, complete it: Hammer Stamp → Hammer Brush → Polishing Hammer. Interrupting wastes the combo.

questions.scenario: There are 4 enemies grouped together.
questions.question: What rotation should you use?
options: Fire II → Aero II → Water II (AoE combo)
questions.explanation: At 3+ targets, use the AoE combo: Fire II in Red → Aero II in Green → Water II in Blue. This generates gauge and paint faster on multiple targets.

questions.scenario: A mechanic requires you to move across the arena.
questions.question: What's the best way to maintain DPS?
options: Use instants: Holy in White, Comet, Rainbow Drip, Hammer combo
questions.explanation: PCT has many instant casts: Holy in White (paint), Comet in Black (proc), Rainbow Drip (proc), and the entire hammer combo. Plan these for movement.

questions.scenario: At what enemy count should you switch to AoE?
questions.question: What's the threshold?
questions.explanation: Switch to AoE rotation at 3+ targets. The AoE versions of your spells become more efficient at this point.

questions.scenario: You're casting Fire in Red and see an AoE indicator appear under you.
questions.question: What technique helps you keep casting longer?
options: Slidecast - move near end of cast bar
questions.explanation: Slidecasting: Start moving when the cast bar is ~80% complete. The spell still goes off while you're already moving to safety.

questions.scenario: Boss becomes untargetable for phase transition.
questions.question: What should you do during this time?
options: Paint empty canvases and prepare for next phase
questions.explanation: Use downtime productively: paint any empty canvases, position for the next phase, and be ready to burst when the boss returns.
